Children should never have to grow up in the shadow of a chemical disaster. Yet across the United States, over 117 million people live near industrial facilities that store and use highly hazardous chemicals. Strong safeguards under the Clean Air Act’s Risk Management Program help prevent catastrophic accidents and protect the health of children, workers, fi...

Mr. Zelden - you are thee #1 advisor to the president on protecting the environment and Serving us, the people. Your rationale for changing the existing RMP is to minimize burdens on facilities extracting natural resources without thought of peoples health? You cannot follow ignorance of anyone, period regarding the natural ecosystem of our world. It support...

I strongly oppose the EPA’s proposal to take away these important safeguards that families need from chemical emergencies. Instead, I urge you to keep and implement the Safer Communities by Chemical Accident Prevention Rule under the Clean Air Act Risk Management Program and restore public access to chemical hazard information on EPA’s website.

Residents everywhere, all across the country, have the right to safety from toxic chemicals. Companies should, at the barest minimum, be held to the utmost account when it comes to tracking air, water, and soil contaminants, as have been documented byproducts of waste facilities for decades. And at most, these companies should be required to seek and integra...

Chemical safety isn’t a political yo-yo. Toxic chemical exposure can cause particular harm to children’s health and during pregnancy, and rolling back chemical safety standards won’t “make America healthy.” Basic protections against industrial chemical fires, explosions, and toxic leaks aren’t something that the Trump administration should keep bouncing back...

EPA-HQ-OLEM-2025-0313-0100.title: Comment submitted by Alliance of Nurses for Healthy Environments (ANHE)

comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: I inadvertently submitted a template as my comment. Please disregard that comment and consider the attached file as my comment. Thank you.
I write with strong opposition of the rollback of EPA protections on chemical spill from toxic facilities. We need clean air to live. It is our birthright and necessary for our collective survival.
I oppose this proposal and demand that the EPA continue to allow third-party audits, stop-work orders in hazardous conditions, and public requests for impact reports from regional polluters.

It's horrifying how little this administration cares about our heroes that put their safety and welfare on the line to protect others. Some situations are especially dangerous and hazardous for a responder's health. They should absolutely be protected from the impact of chemical disasters. Corruption should not be allowed to rollback Biden Era protections.
I oppose any effort to revise the 2024 RMP rule.

EPA-HQ-OLEM-2025-0313-0099.title: Comment submitted by John W. Corwin
